metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `merge_change_409_guard` to the grep regex in infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md line 143 (the journalctl pipe command) alongside the existing merge-path event names.
findings.finding: The runbook's operator grep command (infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md line 143) lists 'merge_detection|merge_change_review|voter_voted|MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR' but does NOT include 'merge_change_409_guard'. The plan says to 'document it in the merge-change ops signals list' (the bullet descriptions) but does not enumerate updating the grep regex on line 143 — so the new event will be emitted but invisible to operators running the documented command.
findings.location: infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md line 143 / What section 'Document it in infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md'
findings.suggested_fix:
Change the grep pattern at line 143 from:
`grep -E 'merge_detection|merge_change_review|voter_voted|MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR'`
to:
`grep -E 'merge_detection|merge_change_409_guard|merge_change_review|voter_voted|MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit bullet to the What section listing the unit-test deliverable (assert event fires on merge, absent on non-merge, in tests/unit/test_review_bot.py) so every AC has a corresponding described deliverable.
findings.finding: The What section describes three deliverables (the event emission, the 'fires ONLY on merges' constraint, and the runbook doc update) but omits adding or updating the unit test as an explicit deliverable — yet AC3 mandates a unit test at a specific path (tests/unit/test_review_bot.py). The test work is uncovered in the What section.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: '- Add/extend a unit test in tests/unit/test_review_bot.py that asserts `merge_change_409_guard` is emitted exactly once on a merge change and zero times on a non-merge change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the `merge_change_409_guard` emit is placed at the top of the `if is_merge:` block (voter.py line 393), BEFORE the inner `try: _assemble_merge_diff` — not inside the `else:` clause — so it fires for ALL merge-path entries including those where diff assembly subsequently fails with a GerritError.
findings.finding: The plan specifies the event fires 'at the point the code enters the is_merge branch' but does not make explicit that the emit must occur at the branch-entry point (line 393) rather than inside the inner try/except's `else:` clause (lines 405+). An implementer who places the emit in the `else:` block would miss the coverage-gap sub-path (where `_assemble_merge_diff` raises GerritError and `decision` is set to a coverage-gap decision) — the guard would silently not fire for merges that fail during diff assembly, exactly the kind of silent path the S2 gap was about.
findings.location: What section / voter.py lines 393-416 (is_merge branch, inner try/except structure)
findings.suggested_fix: Add the `_emit(logging.INFO, 'merge_change_409_guard', change_id=change_id, revision_id=revision, parent_count=parent_count)` call as the first statement inside `if is_merge:` (before the `try:` that wraps `_assemble_merge_diff`), so the event fires unconditionally on all merge-path entries regardless of whether diff assembly succeeds or raises GerritError.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
